subject: Remove helm "stable" repo while building charts
commit_message:
Remove helm "stable" repo while building charts

"Stable" repo is removed on targed deployment by a relevant
ansible task, it should be removed in build stage as well.

Change-Id: Ia89435e73c547e7d1cc4de19d635173307d083f4
Issue-ID: OOM-2591
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Verified in Samsung's lab.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/992/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-review/992

Do you need me for the merge part also?

Actually that's the only part I need You or Krzysztof here as you two are the only one who are able to merge my commits in this repo at present. So yes, merge please, thank you.
Change has been successfully merged by Sylvain Desbureaux

Patch Set 1:

OK, that's what I thought...
So Michal Ptacek, Samuli Silvius, Petr Ospaly, Tomas Levora, Milan Verespej, Bartlomiej Grzybowski, Michal Zegan are not working anymore on Offline Installer?

Maybe we should see how to change the committers list with actual committers also.

> Patch Set 1:

> 
> Maybe we should see how to change the committers list with actual committers also.

Just looked in the mirror and Bartlomiej Grzybowski is still me so can be ruled out from that list :-D
But as for the rest you are correct, they are no longer in the team.

Would it suffice if I just remove them from INFO.yaml or we need some nasty paperwork here with dozens of stamps?

Sorry Bartek, I was pretty sure to have removed your name of the list 😂

according to https://wiki.onap.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=11929094:

Removing a Committer:

    A Committer may voluntarily resign by informing the PTL and sending email to the onap-tsc list.
    A Committer can be removed by a PTL under the following conditions. The PTL is responsible for informing the Technical Steering Committee (TSC) of any committers who are removed via the ONAP-TSC email
    list.
        Demonstrated failure of the Committer to effectively perform her/his duties for an extended period of time (6 months or more).
        A Committer has proven to be disruptive to the project in the performance of his/her duties. (documentation in the form of an email thread or meeting minutes is required).
    A Committer can be removed by a 2/3rds vote of the sitting Committers vote to remove the Committer (documentation in the form of an email thread or meeting minutes is required).

    After notifying the TSC, the PTL requests by email to the ONAP Infratructure Coordinator ( infrastructure-coordinator@onap.org  ) to remove the committer rights for the individual. Please include the committer name, email, LFID and list of repo(s) that the individual should be removed from.
    The PTL edits the INFO.yaml file associated with the repo(s) to delete the committer.


So we should wait for six monthes since last +2/merge or merge request from them.

We can also promotes some new contributors if you want (I don't care -- I'm actually pleased -- to review your merge requests but you may want more people being able to do that)

So far I was under impression that to get commiters rights in a repo a candidate is obliged to have shown a commitment to that repo and with Konrad, Lasse and Krystian as only candidates right now we don't have such personalities. Do you think it would be possible to request commiters right for one of those mentioned just for the sake of having one spare commiter in our team? 

Krzysztof Opasiak is from Samsung as well but I just add him as reviewer for same reasons as I add You.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 

> So far I was under impression that to get commiters rights in a repo a candidate is obliged to have shown a commitment to that repo and with Konrad, Lasse and Krystian as only candidates right now we don't have such personalities. Do you think it would be possible to request commiters right for one of those mentioned just for the sake of having one spare commiter in our team? 
> 
> Krzysztof Opasiak is from Samsung as well but I just add him as reviewer for same reasons as I add You.

Yes, in order to have commiters right, you need to have proposed some commits and done some reviews.
So if you want that Konrad, Lasse or Krystian to become commiters, they'll need to do that for a month or two before asking for a vote.

As we're only four to be able to vote (the other active commiters), I'm pretty sure they would have it.
I'd also like to have more "diversity" in company but it's also hard to achieve...
